rationale: Both independently caught the single most important ground-truth issue (trust_remote_code default silently flipped True→False) and the same latent bug (api_server.py else-branch skipping the global UNSUPPORTED_ROUTES guard). Y edges ahead on recall by also flagging the missing test coverage for the batch-completions rejection path, which echoes the PR discussion's explicit ask to audit/test other endpoints, plus a couple of extra grounded minor findings (dead hf_config assignment, kwargs mutation). Y's findings are also slightly more actionable, each pairing a specific line with a concrete fix. Neither candidate surfaces the hf_config-mutability caching concern or the positional-argument signature risk from the inline ground truth, and neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
